diversity_rule: Each direction must differ from every tried direction in ≥ 1 structural-axis (interaction-mechanism, depth-strategy, sparsity-pattern, or input-modality). Adding more params to a tried direction counts as tactical, not a new direction.

_backlog.name: Sparse Mixture-of-Experts Cross-Network
_backlog.hypothesis: Cross-network depth in DCN-V2 saturates because all features participate in every cross-layer. Replacing dense cross with a top-k routed sparse expert layer (Switch-Transformer style) lets the model allocate capacity to feature interactions that matter, with implicit regularization. Should beat DCN-V2 with ≤ 1.2x params and possibly fewer at matched AUC.
evidence_targets: Switch Transformer (Fedus et al. 2021) routing dynamics
risks: Routing instability on small batch sizes (CTR data is high-row, low-batch)
Inference latency if not all experts fit in single batch
_backlog.novelty_claim: First sparse-MoE applied to explicit feature interaction (as opposed to attention FFN in NLP)

_backlog.name: Frequency-Domain Feature Interaction (FFT-DCN)
_backlog.hypothesis: Feature interactions in CTR live in low-frequency components of the joint feature distribution. Computing cross terms in frequency domain (Hadamard product of FFT coefficients) is mathematically equivalent to circular convolution — captures multi-order interactions in O(d log d) instead of O(d^2), and frequency-magnitude gating provides implicit feature selection.
evidence_targets: FNet (Lee-Thorp et al. 2021) mixing in frequency domain
SpecNet and similar spectral CNN works
Convolution theorem: cross-correlation = element-wise product in Fourier domain
risks: Frequency-domain interactions may not align with semantic feature meaning (high-freq components are often noise in CTR)
_backlog.novelty_claim: Frequency-domain view of cross-network; spectral interpretation of feature interaction expressivity

_backlog.name: Neural-ODE Interest Evolution on Top of Cross Network
_backlog.hypothesis: Deep cross-networks hit a depth-saturation wall because they apply discrete cross layers. Treating depth as continuous via Neural ODE gives a learned-depth cross-network that adapts depth per input. On long-tail users with few interactions, ODE solver takes large steps; on heavy users, small steps for fine-grained interaction.
evidence_targets: Neural ODE (Chen et al. 2018) for continuous-depth
Augmented Neural ODE (Dupont et al. 2019) for richer dynamics
risks: ODE solver overhead (adjoint method is memory-efficient but slow)
Adjoint solver numerical instability on edge CTR distributions
Difficult to deploy (latency vs discrete layers)
_backlog.novelty_claim: First Neural-ODE applied to feature interaction (vs continuous-time RNN for sequences)

_backlog.name: Differentiable Feature Interaction Search (DARTS-style)
_backlog.hypothesis: Hand-designed cross-networks are suboptimal. Use DARTS-style differentiable architecture search to learn the interaction operator per layer from a candidate pool (Hadamard, dot-product, FFN, attention). Search converges to a small sub-graph that beats hand-designed cross on the same parameter budget.
evidence_targets: AutoCTR (Song et al. 2020) — already does this, but uses evolutionary search
risks: Search cost is enormous (1+ GPU-weeks on Criteo)
AutoCTR already showed mixed results; need different search space to be novel
_backlog.novelty_claim: Differentiable search over feature-interaction operators (not just FFN/search space like AutoCTR)

_backlog.name: Calibration-Aware Cross-Network (PAC-Bayes regularized)
_backlog.hypothesis: Cross-networks overfit to AUC ranking objective and produce miscalibrated probabilities. Adding PAC-Bayes bound as a regularizer (not just cross-entropy) directly optimizes for calibrated LogLoss, which is what production systems actually need.
evidence_targets: CTR calibration literature (Badges, isotonic, temperature scaling)
DCN-V2 calibration analysis (subset of evaluation typically ignored)
risks: PAC-Bayes bounds are loose; may not transfer to held-out
Calibration-focused model might trade off ranking quality
Theoretical analysis may be reviewer-hostile (PAC-Bayes dense)
_backlog.novelty_claim: First PAC-Bayes-regularized cross-network for calibrated CTR
